engineIntelligence.h2: Bentley Bentayga Hybrid Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the plug-in hybrid powertrain fitted to the Bentley Bentayga Hybrid in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Bentley Bentayga Hybrid EA839 engine - petrol plug-in hybrid 3.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - documented on EA839 family, can lead to catastrophic failure if ignored.
High-pressure fuel pump degradation - common on direct-injection TFSI units after 60k miles.
Coolant leak at thermostat housing - EA839 plastic housing prone to cracking on higher-mileage units.

cards.altText: Bentley Bentayga Hybrid EAL electric motor - 94kW PHEV replacement UK
knownFailures: Electric motor bearing wear - integrated transmission motor can develop noise after 70k+ miles.
Hybrid control unit software faults - may cause reduced power or charging errors on early-production units.
Inverter cooling circuit leaks - plastic coolant pipes can degrade, triggering hybrid system warnings.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt unit: £8,000-£14,000 (supply only) Estimated from comparable PHEV motor units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Bentley Bentayga Hybrid 17.3kWh battery pack - PHEV lithium-ion replacement UK
knownFailures: Battery cell degradation - 17.3kWh pack may lose capacity after 80k+ miles or repeated fast charging.
Battery thermal management faults - coolant pump or sensor failures can trigger hybrid system shutdown.
Charging port/module failures - common on early-production Bentayga Hybrids, may require module replacement.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK replacement pack: £12,000-£18,000 (supply only) Estimated from comparable PHEV battery units - actual quotes may vary.

commonProblems.h2: Bentley Bentayga Hybrid Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Bentayga Hybrid's EA839 3.0L V6 PHEV powertrain is generally robust but faces hybrid-specific challenges. Timing chain tensioner wear on the EA839 and battery degradation on the 17.3kWh pack dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: EA839 Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Bentayga Hybrid V6 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Bentayga Hybrid, S Hybrid, Azure Hybrid, EWB Hybrid (2019-present, EA839/DCB)
problems.rootCause: Early EA839 tensioner design can lose hydraulic pressure, allowing chain slack that damages guides and sprockets.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides, seals; engine remains in vehicle
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before chain stretch; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, replace chain, tensioners, guides, sprockets, seals
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if noise present; restores factory timing integrity

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EA839 with upgraded tensioner components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if chain damage has occurred; avoids repeated repairs

problems.recommendation: If your Bentayga Hybrid has EA839 timing chain noise before 70k miles, preventative tensioner replacement offers better value than waiting for failure. Once chain stretch is confirmed, a rebuilt EA839 with upgraded components is more cost-effective than repeated intermediate repairs on a £150k+ vehicle.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EA839 engine prices for your Bentley Bentayga Hybrid

problems.h4: Bentayga Hybrid Battery Degradation - When the 17.3kWh Pack Needs Replacement
problems.rootCause: Lithium-ion cells gradually lose capacity; extreme temperatures and frequent DC fast charging accelerate degradation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Dealer diagnostic reset, BMS software update, cell balancing
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix for minor capacity loss; does not restore degraded cells

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty battery module(s) only; requires specialist hybrid equipment
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if only 1-2 modules degraded; limited supplier availability

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned battery pack with health certification, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only solution for severe degradation; restores full EV range and hybrid function

problems.recommendation: For Bentayga Hybrids over 70k miles with reduced EV range, a health diagnostic is essential. If multiple modules show degradation, a reconditioned pack with warranty offers better long-term value than piecemeal module repairs on a complex PHEV system.
problems.cta: Enquire about Bentayga Hybrid battery replacement options UK

problems.h4: Electric Motor/Inverter Faults - Bentayga Hybrid PHEV Drive Unit Failures
problems.affectedModels: Bentayga Hybrid all variants (2019-present, EAL 94kW motor)
problems.rootCause: Integrated transmission motor bearings wear; inverter coolant leaks or electrical faults trigger hybrid system shutdown.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic reset, coolant flush, seal replacement on inverter housing
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses minor electrical faults; not viable for mechanical bearing wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove transmission unit, replace motor bearings, reseal, recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Specialist-only procedure; viable if motor windings intact

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EAL Motor Unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 94kW motor with upgraded bearings, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for confirmed bearing failure or inverter damage; restores full hybrid function

problems.recommendation: Bentayga Hybrid electric motor faults require immediate specialist diagnosis. If bearing noise is confirmed, a rebuilt EAL unit with upgraded components is more reliable than in-situ bearing replacement on this integrated PHEV design.
problems.cta: Compare Bentayga Hybrid electric motor replacement prices UK

problems.group: Hybrid Control Unit & Charging System Failures
problems.h4: Hybrid Control Unit & Charging System Failures
problems.rootCause: Early HCU software bugs or charging port module corrosion cause communication errors between combustion and electric systems.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Dealer HCU software flash, charging port cleaning, connector inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses minor communication faults; first step for early-warning lights

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty control unit or charging module; requires coding/calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for confirmed module failure; limited to early-production fault patterns

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Hybrid System Diagnostic
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full system diagnostic, component replacement as needed, 12-month warranty on work
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for recurring faults; ensures all hybrid components function correctly

problems.recommendation: For Bentayga Hybrids showing hybrid system warnings, start with a software update. If faults persist, a specialist diagnostic can isolate whether HCU, inverter, or charging module replacement offers the best value for your specific fault code.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Bentayga Hybrid hybrid system repair UK

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Bentley Bentayga Hybrid
finalCta.paragraph: The Bentayga Hybrid's complex PHEV powertrain demands specialist knowledge for diagnosis and repair. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand EA839 timing chain risks, 17.3kWh battery health, and EAL motor integration. Compare prices for rebuilt engines, reconditioned batteries, or supply & fit services-all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Bentley Bentayga Hybrid Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Bentley Bentayga Hybrid and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Bentley Bentayga Hybrid Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Bentayga Hybrid's production timeline (2019-present), organised so owners can quickly identify their EA839/EAL powertrain and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Bentayga Hybrid introduced Bentley's first PHEV powertrain, combining the EA839 3.0L V6 with a 94kW integrated electric motor.
keyChanges: Bentley's first plug-in hybrid SUV launched with EA839 + EAL powertrain
17.3kWh lithium-ion battery delivers ~31 miles EV range (NEDC)
8-speed ZF automatic transmission adapted for hybrid torque blending
mainEngines: EA839 - 3.0L turbo petrol V6 (335-340 hp) + EAL 94kW motor (Bentayga Hybrid)

faq.h2: Bentley Bentayga Hybrid Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Bentley Bentayga Hybrid engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt EA839 combustion engine for the Bentayga Hybrid typically costs £6,500-£10,000 supply-only, or £9,000-£14,000 with supply & fit. Battery or electric motor replacements are higher.
keyPoints: EA839 rebuilt engine: £6,500-£10,000 supply-only
Supply & fit service adds £2,500-£4,000 labour
17.3kWh battery replacement: £12,000-£18,000 typical range
Electric motor (EAL) unit: £8,000-£14,000 supply-only
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare Bentley Bentayga Hybrid engine replacement prices UK

items.question: What engine code is in my Bentley Bentayga Hybrid?
items.answer: All UK Bentayga Hybrids use the EA839 3.0L turbo V6 petrol engine paired with the EAL 94kW electric motor; official system code is DCB+EAL.
keyPoints: Combustion engine code: EA839 (also referenced as DCB or TFSI)
Electric motor code: EAL (94kW integrated unit)
Full PHEV system code: DCB+EAL
Engine number stamped on block near transmission housing
DVLA lookup via registration confirms exact code for your vehicle
items.cta: Get quotes for EA839 Bentley Bentayga Hybrid engine replacement

items.question: Is the Bentley Bentayga Hybrid EA839 engine reliable?
items.answer: The EA839 is generally robust but has known timing chain tensioner wear on higher-mileage examples; proactive maintenance extends life significantly.
keyPoints: EA839 timing chain tensioner can wear 60k-90k miles
Upgraded tensioner components available in rebuilt units
Regular oil changes with correct spec critical for longevity
Hybrid system adds complexity requiring specialist servicing
Most failures are preventable with timely diagnostics
items.cta: Find a reconditioned EA839 Bentley Bentayga Hybrid engine UK

items.question: Can the Bentayga Hybrid 17.3kWh battery be replaced separately?
items.answer: Yes, the 17.3kWh lithium-ion pack can be replaced independently of the EA839 engine, but requires high-voltage trained specialists and system recalibration.
keyPoints: Battery replacement cost: £12,000-£18,000 typical UK range
Requires specialist diagnostic to confirm cell/module health
Reconditioned packs available with health certification
System recalibration essential after battery replacement
Warranty terms vary; confirm coverage before proceeding
items.cta: Enquire about Bentayga Hybrid battery replacement options UK

items.question: What are common signs of Bentayga Hybrid engine failure?
items.answer: Listen for timing chain rattle on cold start, watch for hybrid system warnings, and monitor EV range reduction as key indicators of EA839 or battery issues.
keyPoints: Metallic rattling on cold start suggests timing chain tensioner wear
Hybrid system warning light may indicate HCU or motor faults
Reduced EV range signals potential battery cell degradation
Rough idle or misfire points to EA839 fuel system issues
Immediate specialist diagnosis recommended for any warning lights
items.cta: Get quotes for Bentayga Hybrid engine diagnostics UK

items.question: Should I replace or rebuild my Bentayga Hybrid EA839 engine?
items.answer: For catastrophic timing chain damage, a rebuilt EA839 with upgraded components offers better value; for isolated faults, targeted rebuild may suffice.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine: full teardown, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
Targeted rebuild: addresses specific fault only, lower cost
Consider vehicle value (£150k+) when choosing repair strategy
Rebuilt units include preventative upgrades for known weak points
Specialist assessment essential to determine optimal approach
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned Bentayga Hybrid engine costs

items.question: How long does a Bentayga Hybrid engine replacement take?
items.answer: EA839 combustion engine replacement typically takes 2-4 days; full hybrid system work including battery or motor may require 4-7 days for calibration.
keyPoints: EA839 supply & fit: 2-4 days typical turnaround
Hybrid system calibration adds 1-2 days post-installation
Battery replacement requires extended diagnostic and coding time
Specialist availability affects lead time; book in advance
Road test and warranty registration included in service
items.cta: Book Bentley Bentayga Hybrid engine supply & fit UK

items.question: Are used Bentayga Hybrid engines a good option?
items.answer: Used EA839/EAL units can offer savings but require verified mileage, service history and hybrid system health checks before purchase.
keyPoints: Used EA839 typical range: £4,500-£7,500 supply-only
Request compression test and fault code report before buying
Confirm donor vehicle was not hybrid-system damaged
Warranty on used units typically shorter (3-6 months)
Best for budget-conscious owners with verified donor history
items.cta: Check used Bentley Bentayga Hybrid engine availability UK

items.question: Do I need a specialist for Bentayga Hybrid engine work?
items.answer: Yes, Bentayga Hybrid PHEV systems require high-voltage trained technicians with Bentley/PHEV diagnostic equipment for safe, compliant repairs.
keyPoints: High-voltage safety protocols essential for hybrid system work
Bentley-specific diagnostic tools required for coding/calibration
EngineMarket network vets suppliers for PHEV competency
Non-specialist work may void warranty or cause system faults
Always confirm technician certification before booking work
items.cta: Find vetted Bentley Bentayga Hybrid engine specialists UK

items.question: What warranty comes with a Bentayga Hybrid replacement engine?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty; terms vary by supplier and engine condition type.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty standard on rebuilt/reconditioned units
Unlimited mileage coverage typical for premium suppliers
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Confirm exact terms, exclusions and claim process before purchase
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
